creator: tatsuki_makino@hotmail.com
text:
I will add a few more.

I wrote in comment #5, it is used in CMakeLists.txt to check for version-script support.
This succeeds with clang, but fails with gcc.
Switched from GNU configure to CMake will generate libraries without version-script in an environment using gcc. (Didn't some FreeBSD architectures use gcc?)

However, poudriere would not have been affected by this, including the official packages, since the png version upgrade would have removed all packages that use png.
If the ports are built privately, everything that appears in "pkg info -r png" must be rebuilt.
